text:
Olympics 2024athletes have been booted out of the Olympic village after their events in a bid to avoid the spread of Covid-19. The policy change was introduced in Tokyo three years ago and has remained in place in Paris.

Athletes from all across the world have been staying in the Olympic village, which is located five miles north of Paris and spans Saint-Ouen, Saint-Denis, and L'Ile-Saint-Denis.

It is housing a total of 14,250 athletes during the Olympics and another 8,000 during the Paralympics.

And former Olympian Sam Quek, who won a gold medal as part of Great Britain’s hockey team at Rio 2016, has offered an insight into life in the village.

“So this is a bit of a breaking news situation,” Quek toldCapital Breakfast. “Normally at the Olympics you stay at the village from the beginning to the end and everyone stays and then goes home.

“But for some reason this year they’ve remained in place the same rules they had in Tokyo. Because of Covid when they finished, they had to leave the village, reduce the spread, but they’ve brought that again to Paris and I’m so gutted for the likes of Tom Dean, the swimming guys, the divers.

Read more:TV commentator protests Olympic coffee is 'made with polluted River Seine water'



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

“As soon as they’ve finished this week, they have to leave the village and find their own accommodation. So their Olympic experience will be cut short. Because that’s the best part of the Olympics, meeting new athletes, going to other events.”

Quek now works as a television presenter for the likes of the BBC, Channel 4 and Channel 5 and hosts coverage of sports including American football, hockey and football.

And the 35-year-old also spoke about the food offering for athletes, with competitors this year able to get their hands on free drinks, while missing out on McDonald’s.

Don't miss:Alex Yee wins gold medal for Team GB with late fightback in men's triathlon

Don't miss:Clare Balding sparks fury with Olympics remark as fans rage 'give it a rest'

Don't miss:Why aren't Jonny and Alistair Brownlee competing at the Paris Olympics?

“Honestly it’s incredible, the food hall itself is a venue in the village, it’s about two or three football pitches big, and it’s got every food you can imagine from the super, super healthy to the little bit of naughty,” she added.

“Of course there’s a little bit of naughty. There was originally McDonald’s on site for every village, so everyone used to smash that after their event, but this year there’s no McDonald’s.

“So I don’t know what they’re going to do. Everything you can imagine and more, and vending machines everywhere with all free drinks, you just tap your little fob and out it comes for free.”

title: Athletes 'booted out of Olympic village early' and have best part of experience removed | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Parsons
description: Olympics 2024 athletes have been forced to leave the Olympic village before the end of the Games.
